⭐ Health and Physical Education

Music

Discover the joy of singing and making music!

📚 Unit 12 ⏱️ 9 min read

Unit 12: Music

Music is an art form that uses sound, rhythm, and melody to express emotions. Singing is one of the most natural ways to make music.

Lesson 1: Singing

What is Singing?

Singing is producing musical sounds with the voice. It is one of the oldest and most universal forms of human expression.

Why Sing?

ReasonExplanation
Express feelingsShow joy, sadness, love through song
Reduce stressSinging releases happy hormones
Improve breathingStrengthens lungs and diaphragm
MemoryLearning songs improves memory
ConfidencePerforming builds self-esteem
FunSinging with friends is enjoyable
CultureLearn traditional songs

The Singing Voice

Parts of the Voice

PartFunction
LungsProvide air
Vocal cordsVibrate to produce sound
Mouth & tongueShape sounds into words
NoseAdds resonance (for some sounds)

Voice Types (for children)

TypeDescriptionRange
SopranoHigher female/child voiceHigh
AltoLower female/child voiceMedium-low
TenorHigher male voice (older boys)High
BassLower male voice (older boys)Low

💡 For Class 5: Most children can sing in a natural, comfortable range (middle pitch).

How to Sing Properly

Good Singing Posture

Body partCorrect position
FeetShoulder-width apart
KneesUnlocked (slightly bent)
BackStraight, not slouching
ShouldersRelaxed, not raised
HeadLevel, chin parallel to floor
MouthOpen enough for clear sound

Breathing for Singing (Diaphragmatic Breathing)

StepAction
1Stand or sit with straight back
2Place hand on belly
3Inhale deeply – belly expands (not chest)
4Exhale slowly – belly contracts
5Feel the air move in and out

Exercise:

  • Inhale for 4 counts
  • Hold for 4 counts
  • Exhale for 4 counts (hiss like a snake)
  • Exhale for 8 counts
  • Exhale for 12 counts

Voice Warm-ups

Warm-upHow to do it
HummingHum “mmm” gently, feel vibration in nose
Lip trillsMake “brrr” sound with lips
SirensGlide from low to high and back (like a siren)
ScalesSing “do re mi fa so la ti do”
Tongue twistersSing “red lorry yellow lorry” on one note

Elements of Music

ElementDefinitionExample
PitchHow high or low a note isSoprano = high, Bass = low
RhythmPattern of long and short soundsTa, ta, ti-ti, ta
TempoSpeed of musicFast (allegro) or slow (adagio)
MelodyTune – series of notes”Twinkle twinkle little star”
DynamicsVolume (loud/soft)Forte (loud), Piano (soft)
BeatSteady pulseLike your heartbeat

Solfège (Do Re Mi)

Solfège is a system for singing notes using syllables.

SyllableNote
Do1st note (C)
Re2nd note (D)
Mi3rd note (E)
Fa4th note (F)
So5th note (G)
La6th note (A)
Ti7th note (B)
Do8th note (high C)

🎵 Sing it: Do Re Mi Fa So La Ti Do (up) → Do Ti La So Fa Mi Re Do (down)

Hand Signs (Curwen Hand Signs)

Each solfège syllable has a hand sign. (Learn from your teacher.)

Simple Songs for Class 5

Song 1: Sa Re Ga Ma (Basic Scale)

Sa - Re - Ga - Ma - Pa - Dha - Ni - Sa Do - Re - Mi - Fa - So - La - Ti - Do

Song 2: Twinkle Twinkle Little Star

Twinkle, twinkle, little star, So - So - La - La - So - So - Mi

How I wonder what you are! So - So - La - La - So - So - Mi

Up above the world so high, Mi - Mi - Re - Re - Do - Do - Ti

Like a diamond in the sky. Mi - Mi - Re - Re - Do - Do - Ti

Song 3: National Anthem (Nepal)

Sayaun Thunga Phool Ka Hami… (Learn the tune from your teacher.)

Song 4: Simple Folk Song

Morning has come, The sun is so bright, Wake up my friend, The world is so light.

Types of Songs

TypeDescriptionExamples
Folk songTraditional songs of a regionResham Firiri (Nepal)
Patriotic songLove for countryNational anthem
Devotional songReligious or spiritualBhajan, Kirtan
Children’s songFun, easy lyricsNursery rhymes
Pop songModern popular musicAge-appropriate pop

Singing in a Group (Choir)

Choir = a group of singers performing together.

Benefits of Choir Singing

BenefitExplanation
TeamworkEveryone must sing together
Listening skillsBlend your voice with others
DisciplineFollow the conductor
FriendshipMake friends who love music
ConfidencePerform in front of people

Choir Rules

RuleWhy
Watch the conductorKeeps everyone together
Stand stillDistractions distract others
Sing your partDo not sing too loud or too soft
Arrive on timePractice starts together
Respect othersListen when someone sings alone

Taking Care of Your Voice

Do ✅Don’t ❌
Drink warm waterShout or scream
Rest your voice when tiredSing when you have a sore throat
Warm up before singingDrink cold water during singing
Breathe properlySing too high or too low for your range
Speak gentlyClear throat loudly (it hurts vocal cords)

💡 If your voice hurts, stop singing and rest!

Basic Music Notation (Optional)

Note Values

NoteNameBeats
♩Quarter note1 beat
♪Eighth note½ beat
♫Two eighth notes1 beat (two notes)
𝅗𝅥Half note2 beats
𝅜Whole note4 beats

Time Signature (Simple)

Time signatureMeaning
4/44 beats in a bar, quarter note gets 1 beat
3/43 beats in a bar (waltz feeling)

Try It Yourself!

Exercise 1: Fill in the Blanks

  1. The musical syllables for notes are called ______.
  2. The speed of music is called ______.
  3. The volume of music (loud/soft) is called ______.
  4. A group of singers is called a ______.
  5. In solfège, the first note is ______.

Exercise 2: True or False

  1. Singing helps reduce stress. (T/F)
  2. You should warm up your voice before singing. (T/F)
  3. Shouting is good for your voice. (T/F)
  4. A choir sings alone. (T/F)
  5. Breathing from the belly helps singing. (T/F)

Exercise 3: Match the Term to Its Meaning

TermMeaning
Pitcha) Volume (loud/soft)
Dynamicsb) How high or low a note is
Tempoc) Speed
Rhythmd) Pattern of long and short sounds

Exercise 4: Name the Solfège Syllable

Write the syllable for each number (in the key of C).

  1. 1st note → ______
  2. 3rd note → ______
  3. 5th note → ______
  4. 8th note (high) → ______

Answer Key

Exercise 1: 1. solfège, 2. tempo, 3. dynamics, 4. choir, 5. Do

Exercise 2: 1. T, 2. T, 3. F, 4. F, 5. T

Exercise 3: Pitch→b, Dynamics→a, Tempo→c, Rhythm→d

Exercise 4: 1. Do, 2. Mi, 3. So, 4. Do (high)

Great work! Music is a gift. Sing with joy, sing with heart, and share your voice with the world!
